meta.homeTitle: Re-Sound | Recycled by Origin. Circular by Design.
meta.homeDescription: High-performance acoustic solutions crafted from recycled materials. We transform waste into silence—designed for the planet, made in Belgium.
meta.productsDescription: Explore our range of circular acoustic panels: Interior, Solid, and Divide. 100% recycled, 100% recyclable.
meta.aboutDescription: Learn about our mission to create circular acoustic solutions from recycled materials, proudly made in Belgium.
meta.sustainabilityDescription: Discover how we close the loop with our fully circular acoustic panels—from recycled materials to free return programs.
meta.contactDescription: Get in touch with Re-Sound for quotes, questions, or partnership inquiries.
meta.blogDescription: Insights on circular economy, acoustics, and sustainable design from the Re-Sound team.
meta.interiorDescription: Modular acoustic wall panels crafted from 100% recycled textiles. Class A sound absorption (αw 1.0), removable washable cover, made in Belgium. Starting from €387 per set.
meta.solidDescription: Large-format acoustic wall panels for high-impact sound absorption. 1200 × 600 mm, hook installation system, 100% recycled materials. Made in Belgium.
meta.divideDescription: Freestanding acoustic dividers with magnetic modular connection. Flexible space management, dual-sided absorption, 100% recycled. Made in Belgium.
meta.rwoodGrooveTitle: rWood Groove Acoustic Wood Panels | Re-Sound
meta.rwoodGrooveDescription: Premium grooved acoustic panels with FSC-certified wood veneer on a recycled felt core. Class A absorption, natural oak and walnut finishes. Made in Europe.
meta.rwoodPerfTitle: rWood Perf Perforated Acoustic Wood Panels | Re-Sound
meta.rwoodPerfDescription: Classic perforated wood acoustic panels with FSC-certified veneer. Broadband absorption, tunable performance, multiple perforation patterns. Made in Europe.
meta.rwoodMicroTitle: rWood Micro Micro-Perforated Acoustic Wood Panels | Re-Sound
meta.rwoodMicroDescription: Precision micro-perforated acoustic wood panels. Dual absorption mechanism, highest fire safety, backlit option available. FSC certified. Made in Europe.
meta.rwoodVeneerTitle: rWood Panel Acoustic Wood Veneer Panels | Re-Sound
meta.rwoodVeneerDescription: Smooth wood veneer acoustic panels. 10 curated species, furniture-grade construction, EPD available. FSC certified. Made in Europe.
meta.rpetGrooveTitle: rPET Groove Recycled PET Acoustic Panels | Re-Sound
meta.rpetGrooveDescription: Grooved acoustic panels made from 100% recycled PET bottles. 12 designer colors, 3 thicknesses (12/24/36 mm), B-s1,d0 fire rating. Made in Poland.
meta.rpetPanelTitle: rPET Panel Recycled PET Acoustic Panels | Re-Sound
meta.rpetPanelDescription: Versatile flat acoustic panels from 100% recycled PET. Process like MDF, OEKO-TEX® certified, Class A+ emissions. Walls, ceilings, furniture. Made in Poland.
meta.rpetFlexGrooveTitle: rPET Flex Groove Flexible Acoustic Panels | Re-Sound
meta.rpetFlexGrooveDescription: Flexible grooved acoustic panels made from 100% recycled PET. Bendable for curved surfaces, columns, and bespoke installations. OEKO-TEX® certified.
meta.faqDescription: Answers to common questions about Re-Sound acoustic panels: how absorption works, fire ratings, lead times, the circular take-back program, and product selection.

interior.title: Interior
interior.description: Expandable acoustic panels for walls and ceilings. Perfect for offices, restaurants, and public spaces.
interior.fullDescription: Our Interior panels are designed for seamless integration into any space. Made from 100% recycled textiles, they offer exceptional sound absorption while adding visual warmth to your environment.

solid.title: Solid
solid.description: Minimal, high-density panels for maximum acoustic performance. Ideal for demanding environments.
solid.fullDescription: Solid panels combine minimalist aesthetics with maximum acoustic performance. Their high-density construction makes them perfect for spaces requiring superior sound control.

divide.title: Divide
divide.description: Freestanding acoustic dividers for flexible space management. Mobile and modular.
divide.fullDescription: Divide offers flexible space management with freestanding acoustic panels. Perfect for open offices, they can be easily moved and reconfigured as your needs change.

rwood-groove.title: rWood - Groove
rwood-groove.description: Premium acoustic wood panels with natural oak and walnut veneers. Elegant design meets superior sound absorption.
rwood-groove.fullDescription: rWood - Groove panels combine the natural beauty of FSC-certified wood with exceptional acoustic performance. Featuring precision-milled grooves and integrated felt backing, these panels transform any space with warmth and style while delivering Class A sound absorption.

rpet-groove.title: rPET - Groove
rpet-groove.description: Sustainable grooved acoustic panels made from recycled PET. Modern aesthetics with excellent acoustic performance.
rpet-groove.fullDescription: rPET - Groove panels feature a distinctive linear pattern created from 100% recycled PET bottles. The grooved design maximizes sound absorption while adding contemporary texture to walls and ceilings.

rpet-panel.title: rPET - Panel
rpet-panel.description: Versatile recycled PET acoustic panels. Clean design for walls and ceilings with superior sound control.
rpet-panel.fullDescription: rPET - Panel offers a clean, minimal aesthetic crafted entirely from recycled PET bottles. These versatile panels provide excellent acoustic performance for any interior application.

rpet-circle.title: rPET - Circle
rpet-circle.description: Circular acoustic ceiling elements made from recycled PET. Create visual interest while improving acoustics.
rpet-circle.fullDescription: rPET - Circle brings a unique design element to ceiling acoustics. These circular panels, made from recycled PET, create striking visual compositions while effectively controlling sound reverberation.

rpet-divide.title: rPET - Divide
rpet-divide.description: Freestanding acoustic dividers made from recycled PET. Flexible space management with sustainable materials.
rpet-divide.fullDescription: rPET - Divide combines the flexibility of freestanding dividers with sustainable recycled PET construction. Perfect for creating adaptable workspaces and privacy zones in open environments.

rpet-flex-groove.title: rPET - Flex Groove
rpet-flex-groove.description: Flexible grooved acoustic panels made from recycled PET. Versatile installation options with modern linear aesthetics.
rpet-flex-groove.fullDescription: rPET - Flex Groove offers the same distinctive grooved design as our standard rPET panels, with added installation flexibility. Made from 100% recycled PET bottles, these panels deliver excellent acoustic performance while adapting to a variety of mounting configurations.

rwood-perf.title: rWood - Perf
rwood-perf.description: Perforated acoustic wood panels combining natural beauty with precision engineering. Superior sound diffusion through micro-perforations.
rwood-perf.fullDescription: rWood - Perf panels feature precision micro-perforations through FSC-certified wood veneer, creating a sophisticated acoustic solution that balances absorption and diffusion. The perforated pattern adds visual texture while optimizing sound control across a broad frequency range.

rwood-micro.title: rWood - Micro
rwood-micro.description: Fine micro-grooved wood acoustic panels. Subtle texture with exceptional acoustic performance for premium interiors.
rwood-micro.fullDescription: rWood - Micro combines refined aesthetics with high-performance acoustics. The micro-groove pattern is precision-milled into FSC-certified wood veneer, creating a subtle texture that enhances both the visual character and acoustic properties of any space.

rwood-veneer.title: rWood - Veneer
rwood-veneer.description: Clean, smooth wood veneer acoustic panels. Timeless elegance meets Class A sound absorption for premium spaces.
rwood-veneer.fullDescription: rWood - Veneer offers the pure, uninterrupted beauty of FSC-certified wood veneer backed by high-performance acoustic felt. These clean, minimal panels bring natural warmth to offices, hospitality venues, and cultural spaces while delivering outstanding sound absorption.

story.title: From Waste to Wonder
story.paragraph1: Re-Sound was born from a simple question: what if we could transform the mountains of textile waste into something beautiful and useful?
story.paragraph2: Founded in Belgium in 2021, we set out to create acoustic panels that perform as well as traditional products while being 100% circular.
story.paragraph3: Today, we've diverted tons of textiles from landfills and created acoustic solutions for hundreds of spaces across Europe.

blog.title: Blog & News
blog.subtitle: Thoughts on acoustics, sustainability, and design from our team.

circular-economy-acoustics.title: The Circular Economy: A New Era for Acoustic Solutions
circular-economy-acoustics.excerpt: How circular design principles are transforming the acoustic panel industry and why it matters for the future.

content.intro: The acoustic panel industry is undergoing a fundamental transformation. Traditional linear models—take, make, dispose—are giving way to circular approaches that keep materials in use indefinitely.

section1.title: Why Circularity Matters
section1.text: Every year, millions of tons of acoustic materials end up in landfills. These materials, often containing synthetic fibers and adhesives, can take centuries to decompose. The circular economy offers an alternative: designing products that can be continuously recycled.

section2.title: Our Approach
section2.text: At Re-Sound, we've designed our panels from the ground up for circularity. Using only recycled textiles and bio-based binders, our panels can be fully recycled at end of life—becoming new panels rather than waste.

content.quote: The best acoustic panel is one that never becomes waste.

section3.title: The Business Case
section3.text: Circular design isn't just good for the environment—it's good business. By creating value from waste and offering take-back programs, we build stronger relationships with customers while reducing material costs.

conclusion.title: Looking Forward
conclusion.text: The transition to circular acoustics is just beginning. As more companies adopt these principles, we'll see an industry transformed—one where acoustic comfort and environmental responsibility go hand in hand.

office-acoustic-solutions.title: Creating the Perfect Acoustic Environment in Modern Offices
office-acoustic-solutions.excerpt: Practical tips for improving acoustics in open-plan offices, meeting rooms, and collaborative spaces.

content.intro: Open-plan offices present unique acoustic challenges. Without proper treatment, noise levels can reduce productivity by up to 66%. Here's how to create a balanced acoustic environment.

section1.title: Understanding Office Acoustics
section1.text: Sound in offices comes from multiple sources: speech, equipment, HVAC systems, and external noise. Effective acoustic design addresses all these elements.

section2.title: Strategic Panel Placement
section2.text: Not all surfaces need treatment. Focus on ceiling areas above workstations, walls between collaborative and focused zones, and dividers between desks.

content.quote: Good acoustics is invisible—you only notice when it's missing.

section3.title: Balancing Absorption and Reflection
section3.text: Complete absorption creates an unnaturally 'dead' space. The goal is reducing reverberation while maintaining some natural ambience.

conclusion.title: Start Small, Measure Results
conclusion.text: Begin with high-impact areas and measure the difference. Often, strategic placement of a few panels can transform a space.

recycled-materials-quality.title: Recycled Materials: Meeting and Exceeding Quality Standards
recycled-materials-quality.excerpt: How we ensure our recycled textile panels match or exceed the performance of traditional materials.

content.intro: There's a persistent myth that recycled materials mean compromised quality. Our panels prove the opposite—recycled can mean superior.

section1.title: Rigorous Material Selection
section1.text: Not all recycled textiles are suitable for acoustic panels. We carefully select and sort materials to ensure consistent fiber quality and acoustic performance.

section2.title: Advanced Processing
section2.text: Our proprietary processing transforms mixed textiles into uniform acoustic fiber. This consistency is key to achieving predictable, high-performance results.

content.quote: Sustainability and quality aren't trade-offs—they're complements.

section3.title: Third-Party Testing
section3.text: Every batch undergoes acoustic testing to EN ISO 354 standards. We achieve Class A absorption ratings consistently, meeting the same standards as virgin material panels.

conclusion.title: The Future is Recycled
conclusion.text: As processing technology improves, recycled materials will increasingly outperform virgin alternatives. The question isn't whether to use recycled—it's how quickly we can scale.

sound-absorption-explained.title: Sound Absorption Explained: A Guide for Architects
sound-absorption-explained.excerpt: Everything you need to know about sound absorption coefficients and selecting the right panels.

content.intro: Understanding acoustic specifications can be challenging. This guide breaks down the key metrics and what they mean for your project.

section1.title: The αw Coefficient
section1.text: The weighted sound absorption coefficient (αw) is the most common metric. It ranges from 0 (no absorption) to 1 (complete absorption). Our panels achieve αw 0.95-1.00.

section2.title: Frequency Considerations
section2.text: Different materials absorb different frequencies. Porous materials like ours excel at mid and high frequencies—the speech range—making them ideal for offices.

content.quote: The right absorption depends on the room's purpose.

section3.title: Coverage Calculations
section3.text: More isn't always better. The ideal coverage depends on room volume, surface materials, and intended use. We offer free acoustic consultations to help specify correctly.

conclusion.title: Beyond the Numbers
conclusion.text: While specifications matter, the best acoustic design considers the whole experience. Aesthetics, sustainability, and wellbeing all contribute to successful spaces.

hero.title: Interior
hero.description: Transform your space with Re-Sound Interior—a complete acoustic wall system crafted from recycled materials. Fully customizable, easy to install, and designed to be recycled at end of life.
hero.priceUnit: excl. VAT per set of 3 modules

overview.title: Everything You Need, In One Package
overview.description: Re-Sound Interior is a complete acoustic enhancement solution. Each starter package includes the acoustic core material, a removable fabric cover, and the complete mounting system—everything you need for professional installation.

features.title: Circular Inside, Beautiful Outside
features.description: Every Interior panel begins its life as discarded textiles—clothing, upholstery, and industrial fabric waste. We transform these materials into high-performance acoustic cores using bio-based binders, creating panels that perform beautifully and can be fully recycled at end of life.

modular.title: Modular Versatility
modular.description: With its modular design, Interior offers limitless creative possibilities. Create feature walls, cover entire surfaces, or design custom patterns that reflect your brand. The system adapts to spaces of any size.

maintenance.title: Easy Maintenance
maintenance.description: The removable fabric cover makes cleaning effortless. Simply unzip, wash, and reattach—your panels will look as good as new. This extends the product lifespan and ensures your space always looks professional.

acoustics.title: Superior Sound Absorption
acoustics.description: With a weighted sound absorption coefficient (αw) of 1.0, Re-Sound Interior delivers maximum acoustic performance. Both high and low frequency sounds are effectively absorbed, creating comfortable, productive spaces.

hero.title: Solid
hero.description: Meet Re-Sound Solid—your high-impact acoustic wall panel designed for maximum performance with minimal effort. Large format panels with exceptional sound absorption, easy hook installation, and circular design principles.

overview.title: All Essentials, One Package
overview.description: Re-Sound Solid provides everything you need to optimize your space's acoustics. This large-format acoustic wall panel comes complete with core material, an elegant fabric cover, and the full mounting system—ready for professional results.

installation.title: Carefree Installation
installation.description: Our convenient hook system makes installation a breeze. No specialized tools, no complex procedures—just simple, secure mounting that anyone can do. The robust hook system ensures your panels stay securely in place while remaining easy to reposition when needed.

maintenance.title: Always Looking Fresh
maintenance.description: The removable cover makes cleaning effortless. Simply remove, wash, and reattach—your Solid panels will maintain their original appearance for years. This practical design extends product life and ensures your space always looks professional.

features.title: Circular by Design
features.description: Re-Sound Solid is carefully designed with circularity at its core. Made from recycled textiles and industrial waste, each panel contributes to a more sustainable future. At end of life, panels can be fully recycled—completing the circular loop.

acoustics.title: High-Impact Sound Absorption
acoustics.description: Re-Sound Solid delivers exceptional acoustic performance with its substantial dimensions and high-quality core material. The large surface area provides significant sound absorption, creating comfortable, productive environments.

hero.title: Divide
hero.tagline: Snap to Redefine, Split for Style
hero.description: Meet Re-Sound Divide—the first freestanding acoustic screen designed for flexible use.

overview.title: Redefine Your Space
overview.description: Re-Sound Divide is the first freestanding acoustic product designed for ultimate flexibility. Create private zones, divide open spaces, and control background noise—all while maintaining the freedom to reconfigure as your needs change.

base.title: Integrated Base
base.description: Thanks to the integrated base, Divide is always stable and stands freely on any floor. The lightweight modules can be easily moved for optimal flexibility.
base.feature1: Stable on any surface without fixing
base.feature3: Soft base protects all floor types

balanced.title: Perfectly Balanced
balanced.description: Lightweight yet stable. Divide's engineered base keeps your configuration secure without floor fixtures.

magnetic.title: Magnetic Connection
magnetic.description: The modules connect effortlessly through subtle magnets integrated into the fabric. Assemble and disassemble with ease—invisible, subtly processed, yet strong enough for worry-free use. No tools, no complexity, just snap and go.

modular.title: Modular System
modular.description: Decide the layout of your space yourself. Divide is a modular system that adapts to your needs. Whether you choose a long room divider or several short configurations—let your creativity run wild.

features.title: Circular by Design
features.description: Like all Re-Sound products, Divide is carefully designed with circularity at its core. Made from recycled textiles and industrial waste, contributing to a more sustainable future. At end of life, return it to us for free recycling.

acoustics.title: Dual-Sided Sound Absorption
acoustics.description: Re-Sound Divide absorbs sound from both sides, making it perfect for creating acoustic privacy in open environments. Stop being disturbed by background noise and create focused, productive spaces.
acoustics.benefit2: Create focused work areas in open spaces

hero.title: rPET - Groove
hero.description: Transform your space with rPET - Groove acoustic panels. Made from 100% recycled plastic bottles, these elegant grooved panels combine contemporary design with exceptional sound absorption. Efficiency meets elegance.

overview.title: Design Meets Sustainability
overview.description: rPET - Groove panels bring a contemporary touch to any space while reducing noise disturbances. The visible slat design creates visual depth and texture, perfect for modern offices, meeting rooms, lobbies, restaurants, and reception areas.

fromBottles.title: From Plastic Bottle to Acoustic Panel
fromBottles.step1Desc: Post-consumer plastic bottles are collected and sorted
fromBottles.step2Desc: Bottles are cleaned, shredded, and melted into PET fibers
fromBottles.step3Desc: Fibers are compressed into dense acoustic felt sheets
fromBottles.step4Desc: CNC precision cutting creates the signature groove patterns
fromBottles.description: Each rPET - Groove panel gives new life to approximately 60 recycled plastic bottles. Our manufacturing process transforms post-consumer PET waste into high-performance acoustic felt, creating a truly circular product.

patterns.title: Choose Your Pattern
patterns.description: Four distinctive groove patterns to match any architectural vision. From clean parallel lines to dynamic chevrons, each pattern is precision-cut using CNC technology for perfect consistency.

colors.title: Multiple Designer Colors
colors.subtitle: From neutral tones to bold accents, our curated collection complements any interior.
colors.feature1Desc: Colors stay vibrant even in sunlit spaces
colors.feature2Desc: Vacuum or wipe with damp cloth
colors.description2: From neutral tones to bold accents, our curated color palette fits seamlessly into any interior design concept. All colors are achieved through dope-dyeing, ensuring excellent color fastness and UV resistance.
colors.description: From neutral tones to bold accents, our curated color palette fits seamlessly into any interior design concept. All colors are achieved through dope-dyeing, ensuring excellent color fastness and UV resistance.

thickness.title: Select Thickness
thickness.subtitle: Three thicknesses for different acoustic needs

acoustics.title: Superior Sound Absorption
acoustics.description: The unique groove design combined with high-density recycled PET felt creates exceptional sound absorption. Reduce echo, eliminate noise, and create spaces that are as comfortable to the ear as they are to the eye.
acoustics.benefit1: Control echo and improve speech clarity in any room
acoustics.certNote: Tested according to ISO 354 & ASTM C423

installation.title: Simple Installation
installation.step1Desc: Measure space and plan panel arrangement
installation.step2Desc: Ensure wall/ceiling is clean and dry
installation.step3Desc: Use adhesive, clips, or mechanical fixings
installation.description: rPET - Groove panels are designed for quick and easy installation. Their lightweight construction means a single person can handle the panels, and multiple mounting options give you flexibility for any project.

sustainability.title: Truly Circular
sustainability.description: rPET - Groove panels embody the circular economy. Made entirely from post-consumer recycled plastic, they can be recycled again at end of life. We offer free take-back to ensure nothing goes to waste.
sustainability.badge2Desc: Can be recycled at end of life

applications.title: Applications
applications.description: rPET - Groove panels fit seamlessly into diverse environments
applications.app1Desc: Open plan workspaces, meeting rooms, focus zones

cta2.freeNote: Free samples available • Made in Poland • 100% Recycled

hero.title: rPET - Panel
hero.description: Transform any space with rPET - Panel acoustic panels. Made from 100% recycled PET bottles, these lightweight yet powerful sound absorbers deliver maximum acoustic performance with minimal environmental impact.

overview.title: From Bottles to Better Spaces
overview.reason1: Lightweight panels that anyone can install without heavy equipment
overview.reason2: OEKO-TEX® certified, safe for all environments including schools and healthcare
overview.reason3: CNC cuttable, millable, drillable—use it like MDF with none of the downsides
overview.reason4: 50+ colors to complement any interior design vision
overview.reason5: Combine with rPET Groove for a complete design system
overview.description: rPET - Panel transforms recycled PET bottles into high-performance acoustic panels. Each panel diverts plastic waste from landfills while creating healthier, quieter environments. It's the sound-absorbing alternative to MDF—but at a fraction of the weight.
overview.feature1: Made from up to 50% recycled PET bottles

process.title: Design, Construct, Build
process.description: rPET Panel can be processed exactly like MDF. Cut, mill, drill, bend—your fabricator will need zero new tools or techniques.

workability.step1: Precision shapes and profiles with standard CNC equipment
workability.step2: Direct fixings without pre-drilling or special anchors
workability.step3: Create curves and angles with simple V-cut technique
workability.title: Process Like MDF, Weighs Like Nothing
workability.description: rPET - Panel combines the workability of MDF with incredibly light weight. Cut it with a saw, mill it on CNC, drill it, v-cut it—the material handles beautifully with standard workshop tools.
workability.step1Desc: Manual or electric—clean cuts every time
workability.quote: You soon find out that rPET - Panel can be processed as quickly and easily as an MDF board.

acoustics.title: Process Like MDF, Weighs Like Nothing
acoustics.benefit1: Dramatically improve intelligibility in meeting rooms and classrooms
acoustics.benefit2: Create calmer environments that boost wellbeing and productivity
acoustics.benefit3: Reduce sound transmission between spaces for confidential conversations
acoustics.description: rPET - Panel achieves the maximum possible acoustic absorption rating. The unique fiber structure traps and dissipates sound energy, dramatically reducing reverberation and improving speech clarity.
acoustics.testConfig: Test Configuration: 24mm panel on 50mm frame with polyester wool insulation achieves αw 0.95

sustainability.title: Acoustic Comfort, Environmental Respect
sustainability.badge2Desc: Full material recovery at end of life
sustainability.description: rPET - Panel embodies the circular economy. Each panel contains up to 50% recycled PET bottles, and at end of life, the entire panel can be recycled again. No waste, no compromise.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.subtitle: How Re-Sound collects, uses, and protects your personal data

terms.title: Terms of Use
terms.subtitle: Conditions for using the Re-Sound website

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Re-Sound acoustic panels — from the basics of room acoustics to circular take-back.

contact.title: Can't find your answer?
contact.body: Send us your question and we will get back to you with a tailored answer for your project.

absorptionVsInsulation.question: What is the difference between sound absorption and sound insulation?
absorptionVsInsulation.answer: Sound absorption and sound insulation solve different problems. Absorption reduces echo and reverberation inside a room by converting sound-wave energy into heat as it strikes a soft, porous material — that's what every Re-Sound panel does. Insulation prevents sound from passing through a wall or floor into the next room, which requires dense, sealed mass construction. Re-Sound panels improve the acoustics of the space you are in; they are not a soundproofing solution between rooms.

whatIsReverberation.question: What is reverberation and why does it matter?
whatIsReverberation.answer: Reverberation is the persistence of sound in a room after the original source has stopped — the "live" quality of a hard-surfaced room. It is measured as RT60: the time it takes a sound to fall by 60 decibels. High reverberation makes speech difficult to understand, raises ambient noise levels, and increases listener fatigue. In offices, restaurants, schools and healthcare environments the recommended RT60 is typically between 0.4 and 0.8 seconds. Re-Sound panels reduce reverberation by absorbing mid- and high-frequency sound — the range where human speech lives.

whatInfluencesAcoustics.question: What influences the acoustics of a room?
whatInfluencesAcoustics.answer: Three things drive room acoustics: the volume of the space, the materials on its surfaces, and the function it serves. Hard, smooth surfaces (concrete, glass, tile, parquet) reflect sound and create echo. Soft, porous surfaces (textiles, recycled-fibre panels, carpet) absorb it. The larger and harder a space is, the more absorptive surface you need to bring reverberation down to a comfortable level. Re-Sound panels are designed to add that absorptive surface without adding bulk.

whatIsAlphaW.question: What do αw, NRC, and Class A mean on the spec sheets?
whatIsAlphaW.answer: They are different ways of expressing the same property: how much sound a material absorbs. αw is the weighted sound absorption coefficient defined by ISO 11654 — a single number from 0 (perfect reflector) to 1.0 (perfect absorber). NRC (Noise Reduction Coefficient, ASTM C423) is the American equivalent, also 0 to 1.0. Class A is the top absorption class in ISO 11654, awarded to materials with αw ≥ 0.9. Several Re-Sound products are Class A rated: the higher the αw, the more absorptive the panel.

howManyPanels.question: How many panels do I need for my space?
howManyPanels.answer: As a rule of thumb, plan for absorptive surface equivalent to about 20-30% of the floor area in a typical office, meeting room or restaurant. So a 30 m² room will benefit from roughly 6-9 m² of acoustic panels. The exact figure depends on existing surfaces, ceiling height, and the use case — for a more precise number we are happy to run a Sabine calculation based on your floor plan. Send us the dimensions and we will come back with a recommendation.

whereCanUse.question: Where can Re-Sound panels be used?
whereCanUse.answer: Re-Sound panels are designed for B2B interior environments: offices, meeting rooms, open-plan workspaces, lobbies and reception areas, restaurants, hotels, schools and universities, healthcare facilities, libraries, and cultural spaces. They can be wall-mounted, ceiling-mounted or freestanding (Divide), and the wood-veneer ranges (rWood) are suitable for premium, design-led architectural projects where appearance matters as much as performance.

rwoodVsRpetVsTextile.question: What is the difference between rWood, rPET and the textile range?
rwoodVsRpetVsTextile.answer: They are three different material families serving different design intents. The textile range (Interior, Solid, Divide) is made from 100% recycled textile fibres with a fabric cover — soft, warm, available in multiple colours, made in Belgium. The rPET range (Panel, Groove, Flex Groove) is made from 100% recycled PET bottles, OEKO-TEX® certified, and can be processed like MDF (cut, mill, paint); ideal for bold colours and custom installations. The rWood range (Groove, Perf, Micro, Panel) combines FSC-certified wood veneer with a recycled-fibre core, giving a premium architectural finish in 8+ wood species; manufactured in Europe.

fireSafety.question: What fire safety classifications do Re-Sound panels carry?
fireSafety.answer: Fire classification varies by product line. The rPET range is certified B-s1,d0 to EN 13501-1 — suitable for most commercial and public-building applications. The rWood range carries classifications up to B-s2,d0 depending on finish, and the micro-perforated rWood Micro reaches the highest fire safety rating in our catalog. Each product page lists the exact certification, and the test certificates are available as PDF downloads after a quick contact form. For specific project compliance requirements get in touch with the exact classification you need.

samples.question: Can I order samples?
samples.answer: Yes — samples are free for qualified B2B requests. Use the contact form on any product page to request samples; specify which colours, finishes or veneers you would like to see. Sample kits are typically shipped within 5 working days within the EU. For larger projects we can also arrange a showroom visit or send a project consultant on site.

whatIsCircular.question: What does "circular" mean at Re-Sound?
whatIsCircular.answer: Circular at Re-Sound means three things, designed in from the start. First, inputs: we use recycled materials wherever the acoustic performance allows it — 100% recycled textiles in the Interior/Solid/Divide range, 100% recycled PET in the rPET range, and a recycled-fibre core under the FSC-certified veneer of the rWood range. Second, end of life: every Re-Sound panel can be returned to us through our free take-back service, and the materials are reprocessed into new panels or new raw material rather than going to landfill. Third, design: connections (magnetic, mechanical, hook systems) are reversible so panels can be removed without destruction, allowing reconfiguration during the building's life.

takeBack.question: How does the take-back program work?
takeBack.answer: At end of life — when a building is being refurbished or the panels are being replaced — Re-Sound will collect the panels from your site, free of charge within Belgium and at logistics cost in the rest of the EU. We then process them back into raw material: textile panels are shredded and re-spun into new fibre; rPET panels are reground and re-pressed; rWood cores are separated from the veneer and reused. To initiate a return, contact us with the original delivery reference and approximate volume.

recycledContent.question: What is the recycled content of each product line?
recycledContent.answer: Recycled content varies by product family. The textile range (Interior, Solid, Divide) is made from recycled textile fibres with high recycled content. The rPET range uses 100% post-consumer recycled PET (the actual bottle-equivalent count varies by panel size — roughly 60 bottles per standard Groove panel). The rWood range uses a high recycled-fibre core with FSC-certified veneer on the visible face. Detailed material breakdowns and EPD documentation are available on each product page and on request.

leadTimes.question: What are typical lead times?
leadTimes.answer: Standard catalog items ship within 2-3 weeks from order confirmation across the EU. Custom sizes, custom colours, or large project quantities typically run 4-6 weeks. For projects on a deadline, contact us with the timeline and we will confirm what is achievable before order placement.

quoteProcess.question: How do I request a quote?
quoteProcess.answer: Use the contact form on any product page, or the general contact form, with: the product line you are interested in, approximate quantities or floor plan, and your project timeline. We will respond within one working day with a tailored quote, sample arrangement, and a calculation of expected acoustic improvement. For larger projects we can attend a site visit or briefing call.
